The 1 MV accelerator used at the energy
The absolute cross section was determined at
Ep=353.4keV relative to the Rutherford elastic
scattering cross section for detection angles
of 22.5 - 150. degr. A total set of 7 values was
obtained, which lead to a weighted average of 87/+-4 mb
This value has been used as the standard in the
Two Si surface barrier detectors (with an
Supersonic jet target. 15N enriched nitrogen gas at
inlet pressures of p0 =0.79 to 1.41 bar.
The Ti15N targets were fabricated by vacuum evaporation
of a layer of natural Ti onto a 0.25 mm thick
Ta backing and then by conversion of the Ti film to
the nitride in an atmosphere of 99% enriched 15N
gas by resistance heating of the coated Ta backing.
The angle-integrated alpha-yields were presented from
80 - 353 keV, result from experiments on Muenster
The angle-integrated reaction yields were determined
the yields observed at 90.degr. in the experiments
on the 1 MV Mainz accelerator (Ep(lab) = 350-810 keV).
15N(p,alpha)12C reaction have been carried out with
Ti15N solid targets at six energies in the range of
